text.title: Product Lifecycle Core Rules
text.summary: Testable rules grounding Product Lifecycle. Each pairs a binary check with a sourced rationale; HARD rules — user-supplied input cannot override.

rule.statement: Use idea / launch / growth / maturity / decline; no in-between labels.
rule.rationale: Use idea / launch / growth / maturity / decline; no in-between labels.

rule.statement: Stage decision carries low/medium/high confidence based on signal alignment.
rule.rationale: Stage decision carries low/medium/high confidence based on signal alignment.

rule.statement: Stage decision routes to a stage-specific playbook (e.g. growth → AARRR optimisation; decline → graceful sunset).
rule.rationale: Stage decision routes to a stage-specific playbook (e.g. growth → AARRR optimisation; decline → graceful sunset).

rule.statement: Stage reassessment quarterly; sticky stages = stale assessments.
rule.rationale: Stage reassessment quarterly; sticky stages = stale assessments.

rule.statement: If preconditions in AGENTS.md "Applies If" do not all hold, OR any "Skip If" disqualifier is true, do not run this methodology — route to the named alternative.
rule.rationale: Methodologies are pre-condition gated; running them out-of-scope produces noise and burns the agent context budget.

rule.statement: If preconditions hold, execute the procedure in `content/04-procedure.xml` (or the rule set above when complexity is light), emit the artefact specified by `content/02-output-contract.xml`, and validate with `scripts/validate-product-lifecycle.py` before returning.
rule.rationale: Tying the entry rule to the validator script closes the loop between policy and machine check.
